# Engineering Manager

We're hiring an Engineering Manager to lead a 6-8 person product engineering team
delivering a customer-facing SaaS platform. You'll own delivery, growth, and
technical direction for a team of backend, frontend, and full-stack engineers.

## What you'll do

- Run a high-trust, high-velocity engineering team. Set quarterly goals,
  break them into milestones, unblock the team relentlessly, and protect
  focus time.
- Own the team's hiring loop: source, screen, interview, and close
  candidates. Calibrate interviewers and run debriefs.
- Coach engineers across the seniority ladder. Run weekly 1:1s, write
  honest performance feedback, and own promotion cases.
- Partner with Product and Design on roadmap. Push back when scope is
  unrealistic; champion engineering investment when it's underpriced.
- Drive engineering excellence: code review culture, test discipline,
  on-call rotation, incident review.
- Communicate up to leadership and across to peer teams. Translate
  technical risk into business consequences.
- Stay technically engaged — read PRs, weigh in on architecture
  discussions, and write the occasional non-critical-path code.

## What you bring

- 5+ years engineering, 2+ years managing engineers (line management,
  not just tech-lead).
- Strong opinions about code review, testing, and incident response —
  and a willingness to revise them.
- Track record of shipping. Bonus if it's a SaaS product at scale.
- Interview design experience. You've built loops, not just been on them.
- Comfortable with the engineering stack: Git, CI/CD, code review tooling,
  on-call playbooks, observability dashboards.
- Excellent written communication. You'll write more than you code.

## Stack

We run a Python + TypeScript stack on AWS. You don't need to be expert
in every layer, but you should be able to debug across them.
